\"capturable\": capturable,\n        }\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n None:\n                continue\n            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n            params_with_grad.append(p)\n            if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"Adamax does not support sparse gradients\")\n            grads.append(p.grad)\n\n            state = self.state[p]\n\n            # State initialization\n            if len(state) == 0:\n                state[\"step\"] = (\n                    torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n                    else torch.tensor(0.0,    self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in infinity norm).\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n                \\text{ (betas)},\\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)},\n                \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                                                \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm}    \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                          \\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n                u_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( infinity norm)}                                 \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t      \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t               \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}u_t      \\leftarrow   \\mathrm{max}(\\beta_2 u_{t-1}, |g_{t}|+\\epsilon)   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\frac{\\gamma m_t}{(1-\\beta^t_1) u_t} \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 2e-3)\n        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n            running averages of gradient and its square\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        {_capturable_doc}\n\n    .. _Adam\\: A Method for Stochastic Optimization:\n        https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        grad = grads[i]\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n        exp_inf = exp_infs[i]\n        step_t = state_steps[i]\n\n        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n            assert (\n                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n            exp_inf = torch.view_as_real(exp_inf)\n\n        # Update biased first moment estimate.\n        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - beta1)\n        # Update the exponentially weighted infinity norm.\n        if not differentiable:\n            torch.maximum(\n                exp_inf.mul_(beta2),\n                grad.abs().add_(eps),\n                out=exp_inf,\n            )\n        else:\n            norm_buf = torch.cat(\n                [exp_inf.mul_(beta2).unsqueeze(0), grad.abs().add_(eps).unsqueeze_(0)],\n                0,\n            )\n            exp_inf.copy_(torch.amax(norm_buf, 0, keepdim=False))\n\n        if capturable:\n            # why jump through extra hoops and negate bias_correction? check out #121238\n            # once fixed, we should use bias_correction with addcdiv value=-1 for readability\n            neg_bias_correction = beta1**step_t - 1\n            neg_bias_correction.div_(lr)\n            denom = exp_inf * neg_bias_correction\n            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n        else:\n            bias_correction = 1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step_t)\n            clr = lr / bias_correction\n\n            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, exp_inf, value=-clr)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        grouped_params_,\n        grouped_grads_,\n        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n        grouped_exp_infs_,\n        grouped_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n        grouped_exp_infs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_infs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(\n                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs\n            )\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if maximize:\n                # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        # Update biased first moment estimate.\n        torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - beta1)\n\n        # Update the exponentially weighted infinity norm.\n        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_exp_infs, beta2)\n\n        # in this case, we need to introduce a copy of the grads\n        # since one has not been introduced previously\n        if not maximize and weight_decay == 0:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_abs(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_abs_(grouped_grads)\n\n        torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, eps)\n        torch._foreach_maximum_(grouped_exp_infs, grouped_grads)\n\n        bias_corrections: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        if capturable:\n            bias_corrections = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, grouped_state_steps)\n            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_corrections, 1)\n            torch._foreach_div_(bias_corrections, lr)\n\n            denom = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_exp_infs, bias_corrections)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, denom)\n        else:\n            bias_corrections = [\n                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in grouped_state_steps\n            ]\n            step_size = [(_get_value(lr) / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_corrections]\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n                grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs, step_size\n            )\n\n\n@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_adamax)\ndef adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    capturable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs adamax algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adamax` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n    ):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    if foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n\n    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_adamax\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_adamax\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        exp_avgs,\n        exp_infs,\n        state_steps,\n        eps=eps,\n        beta1=beta1,\n        beta2=beta2,\n        lr=lr,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n        capturable=capturable,\n    )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":4,"oldLines":6,"newStart":4,"newLines":7,"lines":[" import torch"," from torch import Tensor"," ","+from ._foreach import foreach_apply_maximize, foreach_apply_weight_decay"," from .optimizer import (","     _capturable_doc,","     _default_to_fused_or_foreach,"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

  def _init_group(\n        self, group, params_with_grad, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps\n    ):\n        has_complex = False\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is None:\n                continue\n            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n            params_with_grad.append(p)\n            if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"Adamax does not support sparse gradients\")\n            grads.append(p.grad)\n\n            state = self.state[p]\n\n            # State initialization\n            if len(state) == 0:\n                state[\"step\"] = (\n                    torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n                    else torch.tensor(0.0, state[\"exp_inf\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                )\n\n            exp_avgs.append(state[\"exp_avg\"])\n            exp_infs.append(state[\"exp_inf\"])\n            state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n\n        return has_complex\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Performs a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n\n            beta1, beta2 = group[\"betas\"]\n            eps = group[\"eps\"]\n            lr = group[\"lr\"]\n            weight_decay = group[\"weight_decay\"]\n            foreach = group[\"foreach\"]\n            maximize = group[\"maximize\"]\n            differentiable = group[\"differentiable\"]\n            capturable = group[\"capturable\"]\n\n            has_complex = self._init_group(\n                group, params_with_grad, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps\n            )\n\n            adamax(\n                params_with_grad,\n capturable=capturable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nAdamax.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements Adamax algorithm (a variant of Adam based on infinity norm).\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n                \\text{ (betas)},\\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)},\n                \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                                                \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm}    \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                          \\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n                u_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( infinity norm)}                                 \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t      \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t               \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}u_t      \\leftarrow   \\mathrm{max}(\\beta_2 u_{t-1}, |g_{t}|+\\epsilon)   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\frac{\\gamma m_t}{(1-\\beta^t_1) u_t} \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 2e-3)\n        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n            running averages of gradient and its square\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        {_capturable_doc}\n\n    .. _Adam\\: A Method for Stochastic Optimization:\n        https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        grad = grads[i]\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n        exp_inf = exp_infs[i]\n        step_t = state_steps[i]\n\n        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n            assert (\n                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n            exp_inf = torch.view_as_real(exp_inf)\n\n        # Update biased first moment estimate.\n        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - beta1)\n        # Update the exponentially weighted infinity norm.\n        if not differentiable:\n            torch.maximum(\n                exp_inf.mul_(beta2),\n                grad.abs().add_(eps),\n                out=exp_inf,\n            )\n        else:\n            norm_buf = torch.cat(\n                [exp_inf.mul_(beta2).unsqueeze(0), grad.abs().add_(eps).unsqueeze_(0)],\n                0,\n            )\n            exp_inf.copy_(torch.amax(norm_buf, 0, keepdim=False))\n\n        if capturable:\n            # why jump through extra hoops and negate bias_correction? check out #121238\n            # once fixed, we should use bias_correction with addcdiv value=-1 for readability\n            neg_bias_correction = beta1**step_t - 1\n            neg_bias_correction.div_(lr)\n            denom = exp_inf * neg_bias_correction\n            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n        else:\n            bias_correction = 1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step_t)\n            clr = lr / bias_correction\n\n            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, exp_inf, value=-clr)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        grouped_params_,\n        grouped_grads_,\n        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n        grouped_exp_infs_,\n        grouped_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n        grouped_exp_infs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_infs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(\n                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs\n            )\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if maximize:\n                # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        # Update biased first moment estimate.\n        torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - beta1)\n\n        # Update the exponentially weighted infinity norm.\n        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_exp_infs, beta2)\n\n        # in this case, we need to introduce a copy of the grads\n        # since one has not been introduced previously\n        if not maximize and weight_decay == 0:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_abs(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_abs_(grouped_grads)\n\n        torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, eps)\n        torch._foreach_maximum_(grouped_exp_infs, grouped_grads)\n\n        bias_corrections: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        if capturable:\n            bias_corrections = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, grouped_state_steps)\n            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_corrections, 1)\n            torch._foreach_div_(bias_corrections, lr)\n\n            denom = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_exp_infs, bias_corrections)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, denom)\n        else:\n            bias_corrections = [\n                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in grouped_state_steps\n            ]\n            step_size = [(_get_value(lr) / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_corrections]\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n                grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs, step_size\n            )\n\n\n@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_adamax)\ndef adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    # else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                    )\n\n    def _init_group(\n        self,\n        group: dict[str, Any],\n        params_with_grad: list[Tensor],\n        grads: list[Tensor],\n        square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n        acc_deltas: list[Tensor],\n        state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    ):\n        has_complex = False\n        p: Tensor\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is None:\n                continue\n            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n            params_with_grad.append(p)\n            if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"Adadelta does not support sparse gradients\")\n            grads.append(p.grad)\n\n            state = self.state[p]\n\n            # Lazy state initialization\n            if len(state) == 0:\n                state[\"step\"] = (\n                    torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n                    else torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                )\n\n                state[\"square_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                )\n                state[\"acc_delta\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                )\n\n            square_avgs.append(state[\"square_avg\"])\n            acc_deltas.append(state[\"acc_delta\"])\n            state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n\n        return has_complex\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            square_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n            acc_deltas: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n            (\n                lr,\n                rho,\n                eps,\n                weight_decay,\n                foreach,\n                maximize,\n                differentiable,\n                capturable,\n            ) = (\n                group[\"lr\"],\n                group[\"rho\"],\n                group[\"eps\"],\n                group[\"weight_decay\"],\n                group[\"foreach\"],\n                group[\"maximize\"],\n                group[\"differentiable\"],\n                group[\"capturable\"],\n            )\n\n            has_complex = self._init_group(\n                group, params_with_grad, grads, square_avgs, acc_deltas, state_steps\n            )\n\n            adadelta(\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                square_avgs,\n                acc_deltas,\n                state_steps,\n                lr=lr,\n                rho=rho,\n                eps=eps,\n                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n                foreach=foreach,\n                maximize=maximize,\n                differentiable=differentiable,\n                capturable=capturable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nAdadelta.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements Adadelta algorithm.\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},\n                \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\rho \\text{ (decay)},\n                \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)}                                                \\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  v_0  \\leftarrow 0 \\: \\text{ (square avg)},\n                \\: u_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\: \\text{ (accumulate variables)}                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} v_t      \\leftarrow v_{t-1} \\rho + g^2_t (1 - \\rho)                    \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\Delta x_t    \\leftarrow   \\frac{\\sqrt{u_{t-1} +\n                \\epsilon }}{ \\sqrt{v_t + \\epsilon}  }g_t \\hspace{21mm}                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} u_t  \\leftarrow   u_{t-1}  \\rho +\n                 \\Delta x^2_t  (1 - \\rho)                                                        \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t      \\leftarrow   \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma  \\Delta x_t            \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `ADADELTA: An Adaptive Learning Rate Method`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): coefficient that scale delta before it is applied\n            to the parameters (default: 1.0)\n        rho (float, optional): coefficient used for computing a running average\n            of squared gradients (default: 0.9). A higher value of `rho` will\n            result in a slower average, which can be helpful for preventing\n            oscillations in the learning process.\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-6).\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_capturable_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n\n    .. _ADADELTA\\: An Adaptive Learning Rate Method:\n        https://arxiv.org/abs/1212.5701\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adadelta(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    acc_deltas: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    rho: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for param, grad, square_avg, acc_delta, step in zip(\n        params, grads, square_avgs, acc_deltas, state_steps\n    ):\n        step += 1\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            square_avg = torch.view_as_real(square_avg)\n            acc_delta = torch.view_as_real(acc_delta)\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n\n        square_avg.mul_(rho).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - rho)\n        std = square_avg.add(eps).sqrt_()\n        delta = acc_delta.add(eps).sqrt_()\n        if differentiable:\n            delta = delta.clone()\n        delta.div_(std).mul_(grad)\n        acc_delta.mul_(rho).addcmul_(delta, delta, value=1 - rho)\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            delta = torch.view_as_complex(delta)\n        param.add_(delta, alpha=-lr)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adadelta(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    acc_deltas: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    rho: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, square_avgs, acc_deltas, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_square_avgs_,\n        device_acc_deltas_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_square_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], device_square_avgs_)\n        device_acc_deltas = cast(list[Tensor], device_acc_deltas_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(\n                device_params, device_grads, device_square_avgs, device_acc_deltas\n            )\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        torch._foreach_mul_(device_square_avgs, rho)\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n            device_square_avgs, device_grads, device_grads, value=1 - rho\n        )\n\n        std = torch._foreach_add(device_square_avgs, eps)\n        torch._foreach_sqrt_(std)\n\n        deltas = torch._foreach_add(device_acc_deltas, eps)\n        torch._foreach_sqrt_(deltas)\n        torch._foreach_div_(deltas, std)\n        torch._foreach_mul_(deltas, device_grads)\n\n        torch._foreach_mul_(device_acc_deltas, rho)\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_acc_deltas, deltas, deltas, value=1 - rho)\n\n        # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n        # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n        if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n            torch._foreach_mul_(deltas, -lr)\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_params, deltas)\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_params, deltas, alpha=-lr)\n\n\n@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_adadelta)\ndef adadelta(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    acc_deltas: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    # delta.div_(std).mul_(grad)\n        acc_delta.mul_(rho).addcmul_(delta, delta, value=1 - rho)\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            delta = torch.view_as_complex(delta)\n device_acc_deltas_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(\n                device_params, device_grads, device_square_avgs, device_acc_deltas\n            )\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # loss\n\n\nAdagrad.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements Adagrad algorithm.\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{12mm}    \\tau \\text{ (initial accumulator value)}, \\: \\eta\\text{ (lr decay)}\\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  state\\_sum_0 \\leftarrow \\tau                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{\\gamma}    \\leftarrow \\gamma / (1 +(t-1) \\eta)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                             \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}state\\_sum_t  \\leftarrow  state\\_sum_{t-1} + g^2_t                      \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow\n                \\theta_{t-1}- \\tilde{\\gamma} \\frac{g_t}{\\sqrt{state\\_sum_t}+\\epsilon}            \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning\n    and Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n        lr_decay (float, optional): learning rate decay (default: 0)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        initial_accumulator_value (float, optional): initial value of the\n            sum of squares of gradients (default: 0)\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-10)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        fused (bool, optional): whether the fused implementation (CPU only) is used.\n            Currently, `torch.float64`, `torch.float32`, `torch.float16`, and `torch.bfloat16`\n            are supported. (default: None). Please note that the fused implementations does not\n            support sparse or complex gradients.\n    .. _Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning and Stochastic\n        Optimization: http://jmlr.org/papers/v12/duchi11a.html\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting these as kwargs for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adagrad algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adagrad` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if fused is None and foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n\n    if fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _fused_adagrad\n    elif foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_adagrad\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_adagrad\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        state_sums,\n        state_steps,\n        lr=lr,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        lr_decay=lr_decay,\n        eps=eps,\n        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n        found_inf=found_inf,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, values):\n    size = grad.size()\n    return torch.sparse_coo_tensor(grad_indices, values, size)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for param, grad, state_sum, step_t in zip(params, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n        step = _get_value(step_t)\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"weight_decay option is not compatible with sparse gradients\"\n                )\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        clr = lr / (1 + (step - 1) * lr_decay)\n\n        if grad.is_sparse:\n            grad = grad.coalesce()  # the update is non-linear so indices must be unique\n            grad_indices = grad._indices()\n            grad_values = grad._values()\n\n            state_sum.add_(_make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values.pow(2)))\n            std = state_sum.sparse_mask(grad)\n            std_values = std._values().sqrt_().add_(eps)\n            param.add_(\n                _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values / std_values), alpha=-clr\n            )\n        else:\n            is_complex = torch.is_complex(param)\n            if is_complex:\n                grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_real(state_sum)\n                param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            state_sum.addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1)\n            if differentiable:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt() + eps\n            else:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt().add_(eps)\n            param.addcdiv_(grad, std, value=-clr)\n            if is_complex:\n                param = torch.view_as_complex(param)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_complex(state_sum)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    # Foreach functions will throw errors if given empty lists\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensorlists = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_state_sums_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensorlists.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        if device_has_sparse_grad:\n            _single_tensor_adagrad(\n                device_params,\n                device_grads,\n                device_state_sums,\n                device_state_steps,\n                lr=lr,\n                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n                lr_decay=lr_decay,\n                eps=eps,\n                has_sparse_grad=True,\n                maximize=maximize,\n                differentiable=differentiable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                grad_scale=grad_scale,\n                found_inf=found_inf,\n            )\n            continue\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        minus_clr = [\n            -lr / (1 + (_get_value(step) - 1) * lr_decay) for step in device_state_steps\n        ]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_state_sums, device_grads, device_grads, value=1)\n\n        std = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_state_sums)\n        torch._foreach_add_(std, eps)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0 or maximize:\n            # Again, reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated\n            torch._foreach_mul_(device_grads, minus_clr)\n            numerator = device_grads\n        else:\n            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, minus_clr)  # decay)},                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{12mm}    \\tau \\text{ (initial accumulator value)}, \\: \\eta\\text{ (lr decay)}\\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  state\\_sum_0 \\leftarrow \\tau                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{\\gamma}    \\leftarrow \\gamma / (1 +(t-1) \\eta)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                             \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}state\\_sum_t  \\leftarrow  state\\_sum_{t-1} + g^2_t                      \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow\n                \\theta_{t-1}- \\tilde{\\gamma} \\frac{g_t}{\\sqrt{state\\_sum_t}+\\epsilon}            \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning\n    and Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n        lr_decay (float, optional): learning rate decay (default: 0)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        initial_accumulator_value (float, optional): initial value of the\n            sum of squares of gradients (default: 0)\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-10)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        fused (bool, optional): whether the fused implementation (CPU only) is used.\n            Currently, `torch.float64`, `torch.float32`, `torch.float16`, and `torch.bfloat16`\n            are supported. (default: None). Please note that the fused implementations does not\n            support sparse or complex gradients.\n    .. _Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning and Stochastic\n        Optimization: http://jmlr.org/papers/v12/duchi11a.html\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting these as kwargs for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adagrad algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adagrad` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if fused is None and foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n\n    if fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _fused_adagrad\n    elif foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_adagrad\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_adagrad\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        state_sums,\n        state_steps,\n        lr=lr,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        lr_decay=lr_decay,\n        eps=eps,\n        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n        found_inf=found_inf,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, values):\n    size = grad.size()\n    return torch.sparse_coo_tensor(grad_indices, values, size)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for param, grad, state_sum, step_t in zip(params, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n        step = _get_value(step_t)\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"weight_decay option is not compatible with sparse gradients\"\n                )\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        clr = lr / (1 + (step - 1) * lr_decay)\n\n        if grad.is_sparse:\n            grad = grad.coalesce()  # the update is non-linear so indices must be unique\n            grad_indices = grad._indices()\n            grad_values = grad._values()\n\n            state_sum.add_(_make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values.pow(2)))\n            std = state_sum.sparse_mask(grad)\n            std_values = std._values().sqrt_().add_(eps)\n            param.add_(\n                _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values / std_values), alpha=-clr\n            )\n        else:\n            is_complex = torch.is_complex(param)\n            if is_complex:\n value=1)\n            if differentiable:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt() + eps\n            else:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt().add_(eps)\n            param.addcdiv_(grad, std, value=-clr)\n            if is_complex:\n                param = torch.view_as_complex(param)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_complex(state_sum)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    # Foreach functions will throw errors if given empty lists\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensorlists = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_state_sums_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensorlists.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        if device_has_sparse_grad:\n differentiable=differentiable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                grad_scale=grad_scale,\n                found_inf=found_inf,\n            )\n            continue\n\n        # torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # [\"ASGD\", \"asgd\"]\n\n\nclass ASGD(Optimizer):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        params: ParamsT,\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n        lambd: float = 1e-4,\n        alpha: float = 0.75,\n        t0: float = 1e6,\n        weight_decay: float = 0,\n        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n        maximize: bool = False,\n        differentiable: bool = False,\n        capturable: bool = False,\n    ):\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n\n        defaults = {\n            \"lr\": lr,\n            \"lambd\": lambd,\n            \"alpha\": alpha,\n            \"t0\": t0,\n            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n            \"capturable\": capturable,\n        }\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n                if len(p_state) != 0:\n                    if not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n                        step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n                        p_state[\"step\"] = torch.tensor(\n                            step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n                        )\n                    if not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"eta\"]):\n                        p_state[\"eta\"] = torch.tensor(\n                            p_state[\"eta\"], dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n                        )\n                    if not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"mu\"]):\n                        p_state[\"mu\"] = torch.tensor(\n                            p_state[\"mu\"], dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n                        )\n\n    def _init_group(self, group, params_with_grad, grads, mus, axs, etas, state_steps):\n        has_complex = False\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is not None:\n                has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n                params_with_grad.append(p)\n                if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                    raise RuntimeError(\"ASGD does not support sparse gradients\")\n                grads.append(p.grad)\n\n                state = self.state[p]\n                # State initialization\n                if len(state) == 0:\n                    state[\"step\"] = torch.zeros(\n                        (), device=p.device, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype()\n                    )\n                    state[\"eta\"] = (\n                        torch.as_tensor(\n                            _to_scalar(group[\"lr\"]),\n                            device=p.device,\n                            dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(),\n                        )\n                        .clone()\n                        .detach()\n                    )\n                    state[\"mu\"] = torch.ones(\n                        (), device=p.device, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype()\n                    )\n                    state[\"ax\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                    )\n\n                mus.append(state[\"mu\"])\n                axs.append(state[\"ax\"])\n                etas.append(state[\"eta\"])\n                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n        return has_complex\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            mus: list[Tensor] = []\n            axs: list[Tensor] = []\n            etas: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n\n            has_complex = self._init_group(\n                group, params_with_grad, grads, mus, axs, etas, state_steps\n            )\n\n            asgd(\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                axs,\n                mus,\n                etas,\n                state_steps,\n                lambd=group[\"lambd\"],\n                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n                t0=group[\"t0\"],\n                alpha=group[\"alpha\"],\n                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nASGD.__doc__ = rf\"\"\"Implements Averaged Stochastic Gradient Descent.\n\n    It has been proposed in `Acceleration of stochastic approximation by\n    averaging`_.\n\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n        lambd (float, optional): decay term (default: 1e-4)\n        alpha (float, optional): power for eta update (default: 0.75)\n        t0 (float, optional): point at which to start averaging (default: 1e6)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        {_capturable_doc}\n\n    .. _Acceleration of stochastic approximation by averaging:\n        https://meyn.ece.ufl.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/77/archive/spm_files/Courses/ECE555-2011/555media/poljud92.pdf\n\n    \"\"\"\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_asgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    axs: list[Tensor],\n    mus: list[Tensor],\n    etas: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lambd: float,\n    lr: float,\n    t0: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        grad = grads[i]\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n        mu = mus[i]\n        ax = axs[i]\n        eta = etas[i]\n        step_t = state_steps[i]\n\n        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n            assert (\n                param.device.type\n                == mu.device.type\n                == eta.device.type\n                == step_t.device.type\n                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params, mus, etas, and state_steps must be \"\n                f\"on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            ax = torch.view_as_real(ax)\n\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        if capturable:\n            param.mul_(1 - lambd * eta)\n            param.addcmul_(grad, eta, value=-1)  # update parameter\n        else:\n            eta_value = _get_value(eta)\n            param.mul_(1 - lambd * eta_value)  # decay term\n            param.add_(grad, alpha=-eta_value)  # update parameter\n\n        # averaging\n        if capturable or mu.item() != 1:\n            ax.add_(param.sub(ax).mul_(mu))\n        else:\n            ax.copy_(param)\n\n        if capturable:\n            eta.copy_(lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step_t) ** alpha))\n            mu.copy_(1 / torch.maximum(step_t - t0, torch.ones_like(step_t)))\n        else:\n            step = _get_value(step_t)\n            new_eta = torch.as_tensor(lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step) ** alpha))\n            eta.copy_(new_eta)\n            new_mu = torch.as_tensor(1 / max(1, step - t0))\n            mu.copy_(new_mu)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_asgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    axs: list[Tensor],\n    mus: list[Tensor],\n    etas: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lambd: float,\n    lr: float,\n    t0: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == mu.device.type == eta.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, mu, eta, step in zip(params, mus, etas, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params, mus, etas, and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, axs, mus, etas, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (device, _), (\n        (\n            grouped_params_,\n            grouped_grads_,\n            grouped_axs_,\n            grouped_mus_,\n            grouped_etas_,\n            grouped_state_steps_,\n        ),\n        _,\n    ) in grouped_tensors.items():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_axs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_axs_)\n        grouped_mus = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_mus_)\n        grouped_etas = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_etas_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_axs)\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        # intermediate = grad + param * lambd\n        intermediate: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n                intermediate = grouped_grads\n            else:\n                intermediate = torch._foreach_add(\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n            torch._foreach_add_(intermediate, grouped_params, alpha=lambd)\n        else:\n            intermediate = torch._foreach_add(\n                grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=lambd\n            )\n\n        # update param\n        # param * (1 - lambd * eta) - eta * grad\n        # => param - param * lambd * eta - eta * grad\n        # => param - eta * intermediate\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_params, intermediate, grouped_etas, value=-1)\n        del intermediate\n\n        # update grouped_axs\n        # averaging: ax = ax + mu * (param - ax)\n        # Note (mlazos): We can't use lerp here since it requires weight to be float64\n        # and our grouping code requires dtypes to match for all tensors in a group (and it should, since\n        # we use the mus in other places)\n        # all dtypes need to match, so we could introduce a cast in a loop\n        # but since this only adds one additional kernel launch, this looks like the cleaner\n        # and faster solution\n        intermediate = torch._foreach_sub(grouped_params, grouped_axs)\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_axs, intermediate, grouped_mus)\n        del intermediate\n\n        new_etas: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        new_mus: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        if capturable:\n            # update grouped_mus\n            new_mus = torch._foreach_sub(grouped_state_steps, t0)\n            torch._foreach_maximum_(new_mus, 1.0)\n            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(new_mus)\n            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_mus, new_mus)\n            del new_mus\n\n            # update eta = lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step)^alpha)\n            new_etas = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_state_steps, lambd)\n            torch._foreach_mul_(new_etas, lr)\n            torch._foreach_add_(new_etas, 1)\n            torch._foreach_pow_(new_etas, alpha)\n            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(new_etas)\n            torch._foreach_mul_(new_etas, lr)\n            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_etas, new_etas)\n        else:\n            new_etas = [\n                torch.as_tensor(lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step) ** alpha), device=device)\n                for step in grouped_state_steps\n            ]\n            new_mus = [\n                torch.as_tensor(1 / max(1, _get_value(step) - t0), device=device)\n                for step in grouped_state_steps\n            ]\n            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_etas, new_etas)\n            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_mus, new_mus)\n\n\n@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_asgd)\ndef asgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    axs: list[Tensor],\n    mus: list[Tensor],\n    etas: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    capturable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lambd: float,\n    lr: float,\n    t0: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs asgd algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.ASGD` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    if foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n\n    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_asgd\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_asgd\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        axs,\n        mus,\n        etas,\n        state_steps,\n        lambd=lambd,\n        lr=lr,\n        t0=t0,\n        alpha=alpha,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        capturable=capturable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n    )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":4,"oldLines":6,"newStart":4,"newLines":7,"lines":[" import torch"," from torch import Tensor"," ","+from ._foreach import foreach_apply_maximize, foreach_apply_weight_decay"," from .optimizer import (","     _capturable_doc,","     _default_to_fused_or_foreach,"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

ASGD(Optimizer):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        params: ParamsT,\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n        lambd: float = 1e-4,\n        alpha: float = 0.75,\n        t0: float = 1e6,\n        weight_decay: float = 0,\n        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n        maximize: bool = False,\n        differentiable: bool = False,\n        capturable: bool = False,\n    ):\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate:
